module_signals.finding: Evidence base is solid for a one-pass live run, with the KEY primary source (Q1 2026 Letter to Shareholders, 8-K Ex 99.1) fetched DIRECTLY from SEC EDGAR (generic web-fetchers get 403), so all Q1 operating metrics are primary, not aggregator-derived: revenue $8,845M (+49% YoY / +46% FX-neutral), income from operations $611M (6.9% margin, vs 12.9% Q1'25), net income $417M, diluted EPS $8.23 (DOWN from $9.74 Q1'25), GMV $19.0B (+42%), TPV $87.2B (+50%), 84M buyers, 83M fintech MAU, credit portfolio $14.6B (+87%), NIMAL 17.8% (from 22.7%), portfolio 15-90 day NPL 8.0% (broadly stable), country revenue Brazil $4,774M/Mexico $1,976M/Argentina $1,698M. 10-Q (StockTitan parse) corroborates loans receivable net $10,735M, allowance $3,865M, provision expense $1,244M (2x YoY), shares 50,697,182. Price $1,763.36 (settled close 2026-07-02) triple-confirmed (Yahoo + stockanalysis + Google Finance, all to the cent). 5-year trend + FY25 (rev $28,893M, EPS $39.40, adj FCF $1.48B) from stockanalysis, corroborated. OPEN ITEMS capping completeness: (O1) 90+ day NPL not separately stated in the Letter (only 15-90 day 8.0%); (O2) Commerce/Fintech Q1'26 segment dollar split is secondary (slides); (O3) no full 10-Q credit-footnote (loan-loss roll-forward) extraction; (O4) Galperin Trust 7.00% is a strong secondary read of the DEF 14A (SEC 403'd the proxy fetch); (O5) the central margin/credit inflection is UNPROVEN (needs 2-3 quarters). Completeness ~66% -> caps verdict at STARTER-ceiling; M6 supports STARTER independently.

MELI is the dominant LatAm digital-commerce + fintech ecosystem -- a two-sided flywheel where a marketplace (Mercado Libre), a payments/wallet/credit franchise (Mercado Pago + Mercado Credito), and a self-built logistics network (Mercado Envios) feed each other. THE MECHANISM: a genuine secular under-penetration tailwind (management: average American makes 41 online purchases/yr, average Latin American just 7, MELI's own buyers 11) that MELI is attacking by DELIBERATELY sacrificing current margin for share and future scale -- free shipping in Brazil (threshold to R$19), 1P retail, cross-border-from-China, and an 87%-growing credit book. Q1 2026 evidences the volume compounding: GMV +42%, TPV +50%, items sold +47%, Brazil GMV accelerating to +38% FX-neutral (items sold +56%, up from +26% three quarters ago), record +17M new Brazil buyers, advertising +67% FX-neutral. This is a real quality_growth_compounder on a real secular tailwind; signal +2 for business/theme. PORTFOLIO NOTE: MELI is a LatAm consumer + fintech + logistics factor essentially UNCORRELATED to the user's BTC/GOOGL/NBIS 'US AI-capex + liquidity' single factor -- its risk driver is LatAm macro/FX (Brazil rates, Argentine peso), not US AI capex. This is the single strongest reason to own it: genuine factor diversification for a one-factor book.

Profit pool is real and the moat is genuine but CONTESTED -- not the untouchable bottleneck of an HWM airfoil. Ranked moat: (1) LOGISTICS -- deepest and most durable: ~95% of shipments touch MELI's managed network (from ~8% in 2018), ~55% fulfilled from own FCs, 199M fast shipments (+39%); LatAm's weak public infrastructure makes this capital-intensive and years/billions to replicate. (2) MARKETPLACE network effect -- real (#1, 84M buyers, items-sold share gains) but ACTIVELY ATTACKED: Shopee is STILL GAINING in Brazil (Shopee Mall GMV 2x, now ~15% of Brazil GMV; SeaMoney Brazil lending +250%, crossed $1B, replicating MELI's full stack), plus Shein/Temu cross-border -- which is precisely WHY MELI must subsidize free shipping rather than raise take (commerce take 21.3%, -10bps). (3) CREDIT/DATA -- powerful (proprietary ecosystem underwriting, NIMAL 17.8% after losses, NPLs stable while book +87%) but UNPROVEN at $14.6B scale through a full LatAm credit cycle. (4) PAYMENTS -- good, differentiated by commerce-embedding; PIX is a tailwind (MELI rides the rail) not disintermediation, though Nubank out-scales it in core banking (135M vs 83M users). OPERATOR: founder-culture owner-operator franchise -- Galperin stepped to Executive Chairman 2026-01-01 (new CEO Ariel Szarfsztejn, a ~9-yr insider who built Mercado Envios), a planned low-drama insider succession; cohesive long-tenured Stanford-GSB bench (CFO de los Santos ran Mercado Credito; Pago head Gimenez since 1999); ~7% founder stake; ONE-SHARE-ONE-VOTE single-class governance (rare and strong for a founder-led tech co). Operator grade 4.5/5 -- capped below 5 only because Galperin is a privileged, finance-trained builder (not the hardship-formed technical-engineer archetype) now stepping back to non-operating chairman. Signal +1 (not +2): the moat is genuine and the operator is strong, but the contested marketplace + unproven credit book cap durability conviction below HWM-airfoil level.

Financial reality is MIXED -- and that is the honest signal (+1, not +2). THE GOOD: revenue ~4.1x in 4 years (~42% CAGR) and STILL accelerating (Q1'26 +49%, fastest in 4 years); the credit book shows STABLE-to-IMPROVING delinquency (15-90 day NPL 8.0% broadly stable, credit-card NPL fell 80bps) WHILE growing +87% -- genuinely impressive underwriting; governance/dilution clean (~50.7M shares, negligible dilution, EPS growth NOT dilution-flattered). THE BAD: operating margin PEAKED at 14.6% (FY23) and has fallen to 11.1% (FY25) -> ~9.6% TTM -> 6.9% in Q1'26; TTM EPS ($37.89) is now BELOW FY25 EPS ($39.40) -- trailing earnings are DECLINING while revenue accelerates. THE CRITICAL NORMALIZATIONS: (1) REJECT raw FCF ($10.8B FY25 per aggregators) -- it includes fintech float; use MELI's Adjusted FCF ($1.48B FY25 after $1.33B capex; -$56M in Q1'26 as the credit book consumed cash) as the owner-earnings proxy -> ~60x FY25 adj FCF, expensive on owner-earnings. (2) The Q1 6.9% margin trough is IFRS-9 provisioning front-loading (lifetime expected loss booked at origination on an 87%-growing book, provision expense doubled to $1,244M) + deliberate investment (CFO: '5-6pp' self-imposed hit) -- normalize to low-to-mid teens, NOT the trough (too bearish) or the FY23 14.6% peak (too bullish). Signal +1: great growth + impressive credit metrics + clean governance, offset by declining reported margins/EPS, thin adjusted FCF, and a bull case that hinges on an UNPROVEN margin-and-credit inflection.

NO permanent-impairment or credit-book SOLVENCY veto: the $14.6B book carries a $3.9B allowance (~26% of gross), 149% coverage of 15+ day past-due, stable NPLs, is funded partly by 3rd-party financing + $19.9B AUM/deposits, and the parent holds ~$15.1B cash -- a stress scenario devastates earnings but does not plausibly bankrupt the company. Hard veto does NOT fire. THE ACTUAL TRAPS (sizing/timing, not veto): (F1) margin compression is PERMANENT not temporary -- competition forces the free-shipping subsidy indefinitely, op margin stuck ~7-9%; (F2) CREDIT-BOOK DETERIORATION -- the one that matters most: the 87% growth was bought by loosening underwriting and realized CHARGE-OFFS (not just IFRS-9 provisions) rise through a LatAm downturn, converting the base case into the severe-bear (this book has never been $14.6B through a recession -- provisions are a forward ESTIMATE, charge-offs through a cycle are the untested reality); (F3) Shopee wins Brazil share (Mall GMV 2x + SeaMoney +250% replicate the full stack on MELI's turf); (F4) Argentina step-devaluation (a further 10-15% peso move per Morgan Stanley hits 19%-of-revenue reported results -- mostly optical/transient); (F5) growth deceleration below model; (F6) growth-multiple de-rate before margins recover; (F7) culture drift under the new CEO. Quantified: severe-bear (credit blowup + growth collapse + deep de-rate) -12.5%/yr; plain bear (trough margins persist, growth halves) +0.7%/yr (~breakeven -- growth compounds absolute earnings); base +24%/yr; bull +38%/yr. FAVORABLE asymmetry -- material upside, limited downside EXCEPT the severe-bear credit tail. Signal -1 (real sized risk, credit-cycle tail), not -2 (no business kill, no valuation-forbids-entry).

At $1,763.36 (~$89.4B mkt cap): trailing P/E ~46.6x (TTM EPS $37.89), forward P/E ~43.3x (implied FY26E ~$40.76), P/S ~2.8x, ~60x FY25 adjusted FCF. 52-wk context: -30.8% off ATH $2,548.50; +18.0% off 52-wk low $1,495.00 (max drawdown was -40.8% on 2026-05-15) -- a BEATEN-DOWN growth name mid-band, the INVERSE of HWM's near-ATH nosebleed setup. 5-YEAR IRR (FY2025->FY2030, no dividend, on NORMALIZED not trough earnings): SEVERE BEAR (12% rev CAGR, 5% net margin, 18x) ~-12.5%/yr; BEAR (18% CAGR, 7% trough margin persists, 20x) ~+0.7%/yr; BASE-CONSERVATIVE (22% CAGR, 9% margin, 27x) ~+16.2%/yr; BASE (25% CAGR, 10% margin, 30x) ~+24.2%/yr; BULL (30% CAGR, 12% margin, 35x) ~+38.2%/yr. The BASE case CLEARS a 15%+ hurdle AT TODAY'S PRICE and the plain bear is ~breakeven -- only a genuine LatAm credit-cycle severe-bear loses materially. There IS a margin of safety here -- in the GROWTH and the de-rated multiple, not in a low absolute P/E. Buy-below: STARTER already actionable at <= ~$1,900 (today qualifies); ADD <= ~$1,600 (near recent trough, base ~27%); fat-pitch <= ~$1,300-1,400 (base ~31%, severe-bear ~breakeven); do-not-chase >= ~$2,500-2,700. Signal +1: price permits new money -- the opposite of HWM's -2. New money = STARTER (sized for the credit tail); existing position = N/A (not currently held; if held, HOLD/ADD on confirmation).

starter_zone: <= ~$1,900 (today's $1,763.36 qualifies). At this price the base-case 5y IRR clears ~15-24%/yr and the plain bear is ~breakeven; you pay a fair-to-attractive price for a de-rated 40%+ compounder with genuine factor diversification. Initiate a Starter (3-5%) sized so the severe-bear credit tail is survivable; this is NOT a wait-for-a-much-lower-price situation like HWM.
add_zone: ADD toward Confirmed (cap ~12%, cyclical-credit-exposed grower -> below the 15-25% Core tier) as the thesis confirms: (a) operating margin INFLECTS back toward low-to-mid teens over 2-3 quarters WHILE GMV/credit keep compounding; (b) realized charge-offs stay contained and 15-90 day NPL holds <=~8-9%; (c) commerce take rate stabilizes >=~20% and Brazil share holds vs Shopee. Best add level <= ~$1,600 (near recent trough); fat-pitch <= ~$1,300-1,400 on a macro/credit scare with thesis intact.
trim_or_no_chase_zone: Do not chase above ~$2,500-2,700 (back near the ATH; base 5y IRR compresses below ~12% and you pay up for the margin recovery before it is proven). Trim/exit on THESIS breaks, not price: operating margin stuck at/below ~7% for 2-3 more quarters WITH rising realized charge-offs and 15-90 day NPL breaking above ~10%; or Shopee overtaking MELI on Brazil GMV growth for consecutive quarters; or commerce take rate falling materially below ~20%. High weight alone is NOT a trim reason.
binding_constraint: CONVICTION / COMPLETENESS -- specifically the UNPROVEN margin-and-credit inflection, not price and not business quality. Unlike HWM (where PRICE forbade entry with a negative base IRR), MELI has DE-RATED 31% into a range where the base-case 5y IRR clears a 15%+ hurdle and even the bear is ~breakeven -- so price permits a Starter. What caps it at Starter (not a full Core) is that the entire bull case hinges on two things that are genuinely not yet proven: (1) that the Q1 6.9% operating-margin trough is temporary IFRS-9 + investment front-loading that inflects back to low-to-mid teens, and (2) that the 87%-growing, $14.6B credit book survives a full LatAm consumer-credit cycle without a charge-off spike. Completeness ~66% caps at Starter; the credit tail (M5 severe-bear) argues for sizing to the tail. Earn the Confirmed-tier add with 2-3 quarters of margin-inflection + credit-through-cycle evidence.
kill_criteria: K-A Credit-book deterioration (the dominant risk): 15-90 day NPL breaks above ~10% and/or realized charge-offs (not just IFRS-9 provisions) rise sharply -> the severe-bear is materializing, the margin compression becomes permanent -> reduce / exit.

K-B Margin fails to inflect: consolidated operating margin stays at/below ~7% for 2-3 more quarters with no path to low-teens -> the 'deliberate temporary investment' thesis is wrong (compression is structural/competition-forced) -> downgrade to WATCH.

K-C Competitive share loss: Shopee overtakes MELI on Brazil GMV growth for 2+ consecutive quarters, or MELI's commerce take rate falls materially below ~20% -> the contested moat is losing -> re-underwrite durability.

K-D Growth deceleration: revenue growth decelerates below ~20% YoY (FX-neutral) faster than the base model, signaling saturation or a LatAm consumer downturn -> re-underwrite the compounding assumption.

K-E Argentina/FX shock: a large peso step-devaluation or Argentine macro relapse craters reported results and/or the Argentine credit book (highest-variance line, 19% of revenue) -> assess whether transient (optical) or a demand/credit hit.

K-F Culture/allocation slip: the new CEO abandons the capital-efficient reinvest-don't-harvest discipline, a large dilutive acquisition, or evidence the credit growth was bought by loosening underwriting -> dock operator grade, reduce size ceiling.
K-G Multiple/IRR breach: price rises above ~$2,700 without a commensurate proven margin/earnings inflection -> base 5y IRR compresses below ~12% -> stop adding / trim.
runner_dissent: My honest call is STARTER, and I want to flag where I could be WRONG in BOTH directions. (1) I may be too GENEROUS: the base case assumes net margin recovers from the 6.9% trough to ~10% AND revenue compounds ~25% for five years -- that is a double-assumption, and if the margin compression is competition-FORCED (Shopee/Shein permanently) rather than a discretionary IFRS-9/investment choice, normalized margins could be structurally lower and the whole favorable asymmetry weakens. The credit book is the genuine unknown -- I am trusting stable NPLs (8.0%) and management's 25-year underwriting track record, but this book has NEVER been $14.6B through a real LatAm recession, and provisions are a forward estimate, not realized experience. If K-A fires, the severe-bear (-12.5%/yr) is the outcome, not the base. (2) I may be too CONSERVATIVE: MELI has run this exact 'invest, don't harvest' playbook in Brazil for five years and it WORKED (NIMAL to profitability + share gains), management is explicitly telling you the margin hit is deliberate, the top line is ACCELERATING (+49%, fastest in four years) at a $29B revenue base, and the stock has already de-rated 31% -- if the margin inflects even to 11-12%, the bull case (+38%/yr) is live and $1,763 will look like an obvious gift. NET: STARTER correctly threads this -- buy some now because the price/asymmetry genuinely works and the diversification value is high, but size for the credit tail and make the market EARN your Confirmed-tier add with proof of the inflection. The single biggest risk to the call is that I am underwriting a bank I cannot fully see inside; the discipline is position size, not abstention. This is the near-mirror-image of the HWM verdict: there the business was exceptional but price forbade entry (WATCH); here a very good business has de-rated to where price permits entry but conviction on the inflection caps the size (STARTER).
